name: TAO 4h Delayed Retest Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: a breakout is not chased immediately; price must retest the broken level and hold it before continuation is entered
thesis: The requested continuation axis makes more sense on TAO 4h than DOGE 4h because TAO carries some trend-up contamination and stronger amplitude. The edge is not the first break; it is the delayed entry after retest proves the new direction is being accepted.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h retests are too sparse, or if retests frequently fail back into the old range before continuation develops, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks structure, retests the broken boundary, holds it, and then continues in the new direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Delayed retests exploit trapped countertrend inventory while avoiding the poor location of first-break entries. TAO has enough amplitude that a successful retest can still leave meaningful continuation room.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it mixes enough trend potential with enough volatility to make retests meaningful instead of flat. DOGE 4h is currently too chop-heavy for this concept to be the primary lane.

indicator_role_map.PLUS_DI_14/MINUS_DI_14: directional confirmation after the retest hold
indicator_role_map.ADX_14: state-change qualifier from range toward expansion

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The market may still be chop-dominant, turning every retest into a range re-entry instead of a real continuation move.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If it trades but false retests dominate, strengthen the retest hold requirement before widening the breakout zone.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower trade count than mean reversion, but better asymmetry when continuation survives the retest.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h produces enough accepted retest continuations to clear screen density, and ETH or DOGE shows a weaker but still visible version of the same grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trades, or repeated retests that collapse immediately back through the breakout line.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because the delayed retest is simpler and denser than previous breakout-persistence theater

validation_basket.why_included: Best basket lane for a delayed-retest continuation concept.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough accepted retests with positive post-cost asymmetry.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h retests mostly fail back into chop.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with several strong continuation runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same delayed-retest logic survives on a cleaner major.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves accepted retests with cleaner, smaller continuation legs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h is too flat and range-bound for this mechanism.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, cleaner retest continuation than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Ensures the cycle's primary lane is still represented in the basket, even if it is not the best fit for this thesis.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE 4h can still express delayed retests with acceptable structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h remains too chop-dominant for continuation-after-retest.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Messier but occasionally explosive continuation after accepted retests.
